name: Tortelloni
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 6 quarts of water to boil and add 2 tablespoons of salt.
2. In a mixing bowl, stir together ricotta, parsley, Parmigiano, nutmeg, and eggs until well blended.
3. Roll out the pasta to the thinnest setting and cut into 6-inch squares.
4. Place 2 tablespoons of the ricotta mixture in the center of each square.
5. Fold opposite corners of each square together and press firmly to seal and form a triangle.
6. Bring opposite points of the triangle together to form a ring and press firmly to meld the ends together.
7. Continue until all pasta is done.
8. Drop tortelloni into boiling water and cook until tender, about 8 to 9 minutes.
9. Meanwhile, melt butter and sage together in a 12 to 14-inch saute pan over medium heat, being careful not to brown.
10. Drain cooked tortelloni and gently pour into the pan with butter and remaining cheese.
11. Toss gently over medium heat.
12. Place in warm serving bowl and serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings
